english: I. the letter ny, double-consonant, distinctly pronounced like n + y (Ssk. ञ), and used only as initial letter; therefore differing in its nature and sound from the Ssk. ञ, though representing it in Sanskrit words. II. symb. num. for eight. III. fish (मत्स्य), nya ₒdzin-pa (nya 'dzin pa), W. *nya zúm-cè* (*nya zum ce*), nya ₒćór-ba (nya 'chor ba) (or bsór-ba (bsor ba)) Dzl., nya lén-pa (nya len pa) (bláṅ-ba (blang ba)) Pth. to catch fish; ₒdám-nya ('dam nya) Ld., an eel Cs; rgyál-poï gsol-nya (rgyal po'i gsol nya) the king's table fish Pth. IV. also nyá-ču (nya chu) (cf. čú-ba (chu ba)). 1. tendon, sinew; W.: *káṅ-pe nya díd soṅ* (*kang pe nya did song*) my foot is asleep. -- 2. col. mark, left by a blow, a weal, *nya laṅs* (*nya langs*) the blow has left a weal W. V. 1. the fifteenth day of a lunar month, the day of the full moon. -- 2. = tses ni (tshes ni) f.: zlá-bai nya drúg-la (zla ba'i nya drug la) on the sixth day of the month Mil. VI. nya (nya) Sch. 1. lock (?) -- 2. muscle Med., nya-bźí (nya bzhi) the four principal muscles, viz. those of the arms and the calves of the leg, v. also the compounds. VII. *nya čád-cè* (*nya chad ce*) W. to arrive sooner by a short cut; cf. also *tad-nya* (*tad nya*). Comp. nya-rkyál (nya rkyal) the bladder of a fish Cs. -- nya-skyogs (nya skyogs) gills. -- nya-krā́ (nya kra) sea-eagle, white-tailed eagle Sch. -- nya-krāb-càn (nya krab can) carp Sch. -- nya-krab-čén (nya krab chen) sturgeon Sch. -- nya-k'róm (nya khrom) fish-market. -- nya-gáṅ (nya gang) 1. full of fish Sch. 2. full moon Cs. -- nya-grá (nya gra), nyaï grá-ma (nya'i gra ma) small fish-bones. -- nya-ₒgyúr (nya 'gyur) = nya-lóg (nya log) 2 S.g., C. -- nya-rgyá (nya rgya) fishing-net. -- nya-rgyáb (nya rgyab) C., earth heaped up (like the back of a fish) on the top of outer walls to prevent the entering of the wet. -- nya-rgyás (nya rgyas) (zlá-ba (zla ba)) full moon Pth. -- nya-sgóṅ (nya sgong) fish-spawn, roe of fish. -- nya-lcíbs (nya lcibs) fish-gills Cs.; mother of pearl Schr. -- nya-ču (nya chu) tendon, sinew; perh. also a large nerve in the nape of the neck. -- nya-dól (nya dol) fishing-net; *nya-dól-pa* (*nya dol pa*) fisherman W. -- nyá-dós (nya dos) a load of fish Sch. -- nya-ldír (nya ldir) 'a muscle' Sch. -- nyá-pa (nya pa) fisherman Cs. -- nya-ṕyis (nya phyis) (Cs.: fish-gills) mother of pearl S.g. and col -- nya-míd (nya mid) Sch.: a sea-monster (this word seems not to be generally known). -- nyá-mo (nya mo) a (female?) fish Mil. -- *nya-tsél* (*nya tshal*) bow-net, kiddle W. *nya-tság* (*nya tshag*) C. id. -- nya-tsíl (nya tshil) the fat of a fish. -- nya-tsér (nya tsher) fish-bones Sch. -- nya-tsón-pa (nya tshong pa) fish-monger. -- nya-ₒdzín (nya 'dzin) Cs., *nya-kúg* (*nya kug*) W., angle, fishing-hook. -- nya-zán (nya zan) a fish-eater, one feeding on fish Cs. -- nya-rús (nya rus) fish-bone Cs. -- nya-lóg (nya log) 1. Cs.: 'a contraction or sinking of the sinews'. 2. Sik.: cholera (Urd. ہیضہ). -- 3. Med., also nya-lhóg (nya lhog), a name for a disease. -- nyá-śa (nya sha) 1. flesh of fish 2. W.: meat cut into long narrow strips and dried in the sun, in C. *śa-bčúg* (*sha bcug*). -- nya-gśóg (nya gshog) the fin of a fish Cs. -- nya-ság (nya sag) fish-scale. -- nya-sóg (nya sog) prob. the backbone with the bones attached to it, resembling a saw.
(Sch.: 'mistress of the house, housewife'?) hearer of a Lama, without being a regular disciple Mil. frq.; nyá ma pó-mo-rnams (nya ma pho mo rnams) Mil. (cog. to nyán-pa (nyan pa)?)

care, nyá-ra byéd-pa (nya ra byed pa) Sch., *nyá-ra čó-cè* (*nya ra cho ce*) W., to take care of, to provide for a person, to keep a thing well;, *nyar go* (*nyar go*) C. for nyá-ra byed dgos (nya ra byed dgos); cf. rnyér-ka (rnyer ka).
weak, feeble, frail, e.g. of a worm Thgy.
1. v. nyá-ga (nya ga). -- 2. v. nyág-ma (nyag ma). -- 3. also nyág-ga (nyag ga), nyag-krám (nyag kram), notch, indenture, ló-ma ṕrá-la nyág-ga-càn (lo ma phra la nyag ga can) having
